title: Deep learning for code review automation
abstract: We propose a transformer model that automatically reviews code changes for best practice violations in pull requests. The model achieves 94% accuracy on a dataset of 10,000 PRs from open source projects.
human_rationale: Paper addresses automated code review, not human recruitment or selection practices in software engineering.

title: Structured interviews for software engineer hiring
abstract: We conducted a field study of structured technical interviews at a large technology company, examining the relationship between interview format and hiring outcomes for 500 software engineer candidates. Structured interviews significantly predicted subsequent job performance.
human_rationale: Directly addresses SE hiring practices with empirical field study of structured interviews. Clear IC relevance.

title: Agile HR: Recruitment in agile software organizations
abstract: This paper examines how agile methodologies influence human resources practices, specifically focusing on recruitment and selection of software developers in agile teams. Survey of 200 HR professionals.
human_rationale: Direct focus on recruitment and selection in SE context. Empirical survey methodology.

title: Challenges in agile transformation at scale
abstract: We report on challenges faced by organizations adopting agile methodologies at scale. Data from interviews with 50 practitioners across 15 organizations.
human_rationale: Discusses SE challenges broadly but no connection to recruitment or selection practices.

title: Technical debt in machine learning systems
abstract: A systematic analysis of technical debt in production ML systems. We identify common anti-patterns and propose mitigation strategies.
human_rationale: ML systems engineering paper with no relevance to SE recruitment or selection.

title: Predicting developer productivity using biometric data
abstract: We use wearable sensors to predict software developer productivity through heart rate variability and skin conductance. Study of 40 developers over 3 months.
human_rationale: Some relevance to SE personnel but focuses on productivity prediction rather than selection. Human rationale suggests abstention.

title: Remote work and software team performance
abstract: Survey of 500 developers examining the impact of remote work on team performance, communication, and job satisfaction in software companies.
human_rationale: About remote work impact, not recruitment/selection processes.

title: Gender bias in technical hiring: A field experiment
abstract: We sent identical resumes with male and female names to 200 software engineering positions. Male-named resumes received 30% more callbacks.
human_rationale: Directly addresses bias in SE hiring process. Strong EC/IC relevance.

title: A study of team dynamics in open source projects
abstract: NOT AVAILABLE
human_rationale: Missing abstract prevents any meaningful assessment of relevance to SE recruitment.

title: Machine learning for automated resume screening
abstract: We develop a BERT-based model for automated resume screening in tech companies, evaluating fairness across demographic groups. Dataset of 50,000 resumes.
human_rationale: Directly addresses automated screening in tech hiring with fairness evaluation.

title: Software engineering education: Curriculum design
abstract: This paper proposes a new curriculum for software engineering education at the undergraduate level, incorporating industry partnerships.
human_rationale: SE education paper with no connection to hiring or selection practices.

title: The effect of pair programming on code quality
abstract: Controlled experiment with 60 professional developers examining the effect of pair programming versus solo programming on code quality.
human_rationale: Software development methodology study. No relevance to recruitment/selection.

title: Personality traits and job performance of IT professionals
abstract: Meta-analysis of 50 studies examining the relationship between Big Five personality traits and job performance in IT roles.
human_rationale: Meta-analysis of personality-job performance link in IT. Relevant to personnel selection.

title: Blockchain for supply chain management
abstract: A systematic review of blockchain applications in supply chain management, identifying key challenges and research opportunities.
human_rationale: Blockchain supply chain paper with zero relevance to SE recruitment.

title: Diversity initiatives in tech companies
abstract: We analyze diversity and inclusion programs at 30 major technology companies, measuring their impact on hiring demographics and retention.
human_rationale: Addresses diversity in tech hiring. Relevant to inclusive recruitment practices.

title: A survey of API usability studies
abstract: Comprehensive survey of 80 papers on API usability evaluation methods, tools, and best practices.
human_rationale: API usability research with no connection to personnel selection.

title: The impact of AI assistants on developer workflow
abstract: Mixed-methods study of how GitHub Copilot affects developer productivity, code quality, and job satisfaction among 100 professional developers.
human_rationale: AI tools impact study. No recruitment/selection relevance.

title: Burnout and turnover in software engineering
abstract: Longitudinal study of burnout causes and turnover intentions among 300 software engineers over 2 years.
human_rationale: Related to SE workforce but addresses retention rather than selection. Borderline relevance.

title: Competency-based hiring in tech startups
abstract: Case study of 10 tech startups implementing competency-based hiring frameworks for software engineering positions.
human_rationale: Directly about competency-based hiring frameworks for SE positions.

title: Agile methodology adoption in government IT
abstract: Report on challenges and lessons learned from adopting agile methodologies in a large government IT department.
human_rationale: Government IT agile adoption. No hiring/selection component.

title: Emotional intelligence and team performance in software development
abstract: Study measuring emotional intelligence of 80 developers and correlating with team performance metrics in agile teams.
human_rationale: EI measurement in SE teams is tangentially relevant but primary focus is team performance not selection.

title: Algorithmic fairness in employment decisions
abstract: Comprehensive review of algorithmic fairness considerations in automated employment decision systems, including resume screening and candidate ranking.
human_rationale: Directly addresses fairness in automated hiring decisions. Strong EC/IC relevance.

title: Containerization strategies for microservices
abstract: Technical comparison of Docker, containerd, and Podman for deploying microservices architectures.
human_rationale: DevOps/infrastructure paper. No SE recruitment relevance.

title: Knowledge loss in software teams: Causes and mitigation
abstract: Study of knowledge loss when developers leave software teams, based on interviews at 5 companies.
human_rationale: Related to SE workforce but focuses on knowledge management, not selection/hiring.

title: The effectiveness of take-home coding assessments
abstract: We compare take-home coding assignments versus live coding interviews for predicting job performance of 200 software engineers hired over 2 years.
human_rationale: Directly evaluates SE hiring assessment methods with predictive validity data.
